mappings.rationaleAI: Genetic determinism covers the nature-nurture controversy, eugenics history, and gene-environment interaction -- directly relevant to environmental modification of the human species and deliberate selective measures (eugenics).

Evolutionary psychology covers the evolution of language, the Environment of Evolutionary Adaptedness, modular theories of mind, and the nature-nurture debate -- directly relevant to behavioral capacity as the basis of unique human culture and environmental modification of the species.

History as Science covers domesticable plants and animals as drivers of civilizational development and humanity's ecological modification of environments -- directly relevant to human influence on the environment and domestication of organisms.

The concept of nature covers humanity's relationship to the natural world, the nature-culture distinction, and ecological thinking -- directly relevant to the qualities that set humans apart and human influence on the environment.
The domestication of horses approximately 4000 BCE and its transformative impact on warfare, transportation, and civilization is directly relevant to the domestication and cultivation of animals.

Linnaeus developed the system for classifying organisms and his work on plant classification relates directly to the scientific framework for understanding domesticated and cultivated plants and their distribution.

mappings.episodeTitle: Darwin: On the Origin of Species
mappings.rationaleAI: Darwin's Origin of Species placed humanity within the natural order, directly relevant to understanding the qualities distinguishing humans in the biosphere and behavioral capacity as the basis of human culture.

Darwin's post-Origin work on orchid pollination and earthworms illustrates human scientific investigation of organisms' roles in the biosphere, and sexual selection in The Descent of Man is relevant to distinctive human behavioural capacities.
